title: Minnesota Sues Trump Administration for Withholding Evidence in Three ICE Shootings
excerpt: Minnesota and Hennepin County filed a federal lawsuit accusing the Trump administration of categorically withholding evidence — including a victim's car and agents' personnel files — needed to investigate three shootings by federal officers that killed two U.S. citizens.
content:
On March 24, 2026, Minnesota and Hennepin County sued the Trump administration in federal court, demanding access to evidence the state said was necessary to independently investigate three shootings by federal immigration officers during Operation Metro Surge — including the deaths of Renee Good and Alex Pretti.

Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty stated the federal government had 'adopted a policy of categorically withholding evidence,' calling the practice unprecedented and alarming. The lawsuit alleged the federal government reneged on its promise to cooperate with state investigations and was actively preventing accountability.

  • Evidence withheld by the federal government included: Renee Good's car (described as shrink-wrapped in an FBI warehouse and never examined), ICE agent Jonathan Ross's gun, shell casings, and information about officers' training and personnel files.
  • At the Pretti shooting, state investigators said federal authorities physically blocked them from accessing the scene.
  • The lawsuit stated the federal government cannot 'withhold investigative evidence for the purpose of shielding law enforcement officers from scrutiny where a State is investigating serious potential violations of its criminal laws.'
  • On approximately April 9, 2026, a federal judge ordered federal agencies to produce evidence related to Good's killing within three weeks — though that evidence was not to be made public.
  • County Attorney Moriarty said her office was considering prosecuting officers without federal cooperation, potentially overcoming a federal immunity defense.
Moriarty: 'When was he trained? What was he trained to do? What's in his personnel file? That's true of every agent that was involved in these shootings.'
mediaPlaceholder: Federal court filing from Minnesota and Hennepin County detailing withheld evidence list and legal arguments
